source: Though precise details of the earthquake are still unclear, the US Geological Survey has said the tremor just after 0800 local time (0000 UTC) was 10km (6 mi) beneath the sea bed.
domestication: Walaupun butiran terperinci gempa bumi masih tidak jelas, Kajian Geologi Amerika Syarikat berkata gegaran selepas 0800 waktu tempatan (0000 UTC) adalah 10 kilometer (6 batu) di bawah dasar laut.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for the Malay translation, ranging from more literal to more natural sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Walaupun butiran tepat mengenai gempa bumi itu masih tidak jelas, Survei Geologi Amerika Syarikat (USGS) melaporkan bahawa gegaran itu berlaku sejurus selepas pukul 8:00 pagi waktu tempatan (00:00 UTC) pada kedalaman 10km (6 batu) di bawah dasar laut."

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Butiran tepat mengenai gempa bumi masih belum diketahui, namun menurut Survei Geologi Amerika Syarikat (USGS), gegaran itu berlaku kira-kira pukul 8:00 pagi waktu tempatan (00:00 UTC) dengan kedalaman 10km (6 batu) di bawah permukaan laut."

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise):**

"Survei Geologi Amerika Syarikat (USGS) melaporkan gempa bumi itu berlaku kira-kira pukul 8:00 pagi waktu tempatan (00:00 UTC) pada kedalaman 10km (6 batu) di bawah dasar laut, walaupun butiran lanjut masih tidak jelas."

**Explanation of choices:**

* **"Gempa bumi"** - Earthquake
* **"Butiran tepat" / "Butiran lanjut"** - Precise details
* **"Masih tidak jelas" / "Belum diketahui"** - Still unclear / Not yet known
* **"Survei Geologi Amerika Syarikat (USGS)"** - US Geological Survey (using the abbreviation is common)
* **"Gegaran"** - Tremor
* **"Sejurus selepas" / "Kira-kira pukul"** - Just after / Approximately at
* **"Waktu tempatan"** - Local time
* **"Dasar laut" / "Permukaan laut"** - Sea bed / Sea surface
* **"Kedalaman"** - Depth
* **"Batu"** - Miles (keeping the original unit)



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2** as it sounds the most natural in Malay while still accurately conveying the information.  It flows better and uses common phrasing.

**Option 3 (More formal):**

**Breakdown of the translation:**

* **In the fastest journey to the South Pole:**  *Dalam perjalanan terpantas ke Kutub Selatan* (or *Perjalanan terpantas ke Kutub Selatan*)
* **(600 nautical mile journey):** *(perjalanan sejauh 600 batu nautika)*  or *(dengan jarak 600 batu nautika)*
* **she completed:** *beliau menyempurnakannya* (or *beliau menyelesaikannya* or *beliau telah melengkapkan*) - "Beliau" is the polite/formal pronoun for "she".
* **solo:** *secara solo* or *seorang diri*
* **in just:** *dalam masa hanya* or *dengan* (depending on the phrasing)
* **39 days, 9 hours and 33 minutes:** *39 hari, 9 jam dan 33 minit*



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 1** as it's the most natural and commonly used phrasing in Malay.  However, the best option will depend on the context of the surrounding text.

source: The questions concerned, among other things, the next chief-executive of Hong Kong, China's attitudes toward Taiwan, whether China has growing problems concerning spoilt children, the growing human rights question, and whether China will economically overtake America in 20 years time.
domestication: Soalan-soalan yang dibincangkan, di antara yang lain, adalah ketua eksekutif Hong Kong yang baru, pendirian China terhadap Taiwan, sama ada China mempunyai masalah yang semakin berat berkenaan anak-anak yang terlalu dimanjakan, persoalan hak-hak asasi manusia yang semakin meningkat, dan sama ada China akan memintas Amerika dari segi ekonomi dalam masa 20 tahun.
foreignization:
Here's a translation of the text into Malay:

**Soalan-soalan yang dibangkitkan meliputi, antara lain, mengenai ketua eksekutif Hong Kong yang akan datang, sikap China terhadap Taiwan, sama ada China menghadapi masalah yang semakin meningkat berkaitan dengan anak-anak yang manja, isu hak asasi manusia yang semakin membimbangkan, dan sama ada China akan mengatasi Amerika dari segi ekonomi dalam masa 20 tahun akan datang.**

Here's a breakdown of the choices made and why:

* **Soalan-soalan yang dibangkitkan:** "The questions concerned" -  This translates to "The questions that were raised/brought up".
* **meliputi:** "concerned" - meaning to cover or include.
* **antaranya lain:** "among other things" - standard Malay phrase for this.
* **mengenai:** "about" or "regarding"
* **ketua eksekutif:** "chief-executive" - standard Malay translation for this title.
* **Hong Kong yang akan datang:** "next Hong Kong" -  "yang akan datang" means "coming" or "next".
* **sikap:** "attitudes"
* **sama ada:** "whether"
* **menghadapi masalah yang semakin meningkat:** "has growing problems" -  "menghadapi" means "facing" and "semakin meningkat" means "increasingly".
* **berkaitan dengan:** "concerning" - meaning "related to".
* **anak-anak yang manja:** "spoilt children" -  "manja" is the Malay word for "spoilt".
* **isu hak asasi manusia yang semakin membimbangkan:** "growing human rights question" - "isu" means "issue", "hak asasi manusia" is "human rights", and "semakin membimbangkan" means "increasingly concerning".
* **mengatasi:** "overtake"
* **dari segi ekonomi:** "economically" - "dari segi" means "in terms of".
* **dalam masa 20 tahun akan datang:** "in 20 years time" - "dalam masa" means "within" or "in", "tahun" means "years", and "akan datang" means "coming" or "future".

* **bahawa:** that (more formal than implied in Option 1)
* **insiden tembakan itu telah berlaku:** the shooting incident took place (slightly more descriptive)

I recommend **Option 1** unless you have specific context suggesting a more formal or conversational tone.

**Option 2 (More Figurative - closer to the idiom):**

*   **mencetuskan:** to ignite, to cause, to spark (more directly translates to "fan the fire" in the sense of starting or worsening something)
*   **keadaan yang lebih buruk:** a worse situation

**Option 3 (Another Figurative Option):**

"Jika mereka tidak melakukannya dengan betul, mereka akan mengeruhkan keadaan," kata Iinuma.

**Which one to use?**

*   **Option 2** is closer to the original idiom but might sound slightly more formal.
*   **Option 3** is also good and emphasizes the complicating effect.

Therefore, I recommend **Option 1: "Jika mereka tidak melakukannya dengan betul, mereka akan menambahkan masalah," kata Iinuma.**  It's clear, natural, and conveys the intended meaning effectively.

**Which one to use?**

I recommend **Option 1** as the most natural and widely understood translation.

source: The volcano had erupted four times on Friday, billowing ash up to 51,000 feet up into the air.
domestication: Gunung berapi itu meletus empat kali pada hari Jumaat, mengembangkan abu sehingga 51,000 kaki keatas udara.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "The volcano had erupted four times on Friday, billowing ash up to 51,000 feet up into the air" into Malay, with slight variations in nuance:

**Option 1 (Most Common/Neutral):**

* **Gunung berapi itu telah meletus empat kali pada hari Jumaat, mengeluarkan abu sehingga ketinggian 51,000 kaki ke udara.**

   * *Gunung berapi itu* = The volcano
   * *telah meletus* = had erupted
   * *empat kali* = four times
   * *pada hari Jumaat* = on Friday
   * *mengeluarkan abu* = billowing ash / emitting ash
   * *sehingga ketinggian* = up to a height of
   * *51,000 kaki* = 51,000 feet
   * *ke udara* = into the air

**Option 2 (Slightly more descriptive - emphasizes the cloud of ash):**

* **Gunung berapi itu meletus empat kali pada hari Jumaat, dengan awan abu yang menjulang sehingga 51,000 kaki ke udara.**

   * *dengan awan abu yang menjulang* = with a cloud of ash that soared/billowed

**Option 3 (More formal/news report style):**

* **Letusan gunung berapi itu telah berlaku empat kali pada hari Jumaat, disertai dengan limpahan abu setinggi 51,000 kaki ke atmosfera.**

   * *Letusan gunung berapi itu* = The volcanic eruption
   * *telah berlaku* = had occurred
   * *disertai dengan limpahan abu* = accompanied by an overflow of ash
   * *ke atmosfera* = into the atmosphere (more precise than "ke udara")

**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 is the most straightforward and commonly used translation.**  It's suitable for most contexts.  Option 2 is good if you want to emphasize the visual image of the ash cloud. Option 3 is best for formal reporting.

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

**Which option is best?**

Option 1 is generally the most natural and widely understood.  Option 2 is a bit more formal, and Option 3 is more concise but might sound slightly less natural depending on context.

Therefore, I recommend:

**"Serangan pesawat tempur AC-130 membunuh sejumlah awam yang tidak diketahui, dengan laporan yang berbeza menyebut angka 4, 19 atau 31."**

source: "The rights of Muslims all over the world are violated by this blaspheming movie."
domestication: "Hak-hak umat Islam di seluruh dunia sedang dilanggar oleh filem yang mendorong kepada kekufuran ini".
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "The rights of Muslims all over the world are violated by this blaspheming movie," with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Hak orang Islam di seluruh dunia telah dilanggar oleh filem yang menghina ini."

* **Hak:** Rights
* **orang Islam:** Muslims (literally "people of Islam")
* **di seluruh dunia:** all over the world
* **telah dilanggar:** have been violated (passive voice)
* **oleh:** by
* **filem:** movie
* **yang menghina:** that blasphemes/insults/defames (a relative clause describing the movie)
* **ini:** this

**Option 2 (Slightly stronger, emphasizing offense):**

"Filem yang menghina ini melanggar hak-hak umat Islam di seluruh dunia."

* **umat Islam:**  A more collective term for the Muslim community. This sounds slightly more formal.
* **melanggar:** violates (active voice - a bit more direct)
* **hak-hak:** rights (using the reduplication "hak-hak" emphasizes the multiple rights being violated)

**Option 3 (More emphatic, using "mengherdik"):**

"Filem yang mengherdik ini telah mencabul hak orang Islam di seluruh dunia."

* **mengherdik:**  This word is stronger than "menghina" - it implies a harsh, disrespectful insult.  Use with caution as it carries more weight.
* **mencabul:** to violate, to trespass upon (often used in a moral or ethical sense)

**Which one to use?**

* **Option 1** is the safest and most generally applicable.
* **Option 2** is good if you want to emphasize the collective impact on the Muslim community.
* **Option 3** is strongest, but should be used carefully depending on the context, as "mengherdik" is a strong term.



Therefore, **I recommend Option 1: "Hak orang Islam di seluruh dunia telah dilanggar oleh filem yang menghina ini."** as the most accurate and neutral translation.

source: BP released their report into the causes of the Deepwater Horizon disaster earlier this year on Wednesday, and shifted much of the blame for the explosion and subsequent oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico, the largest accidental marine oil spill in the history of the petroleum industry, onto , the company managing the rig.
domestication: BP telah mengeluarkan laporan mengenai sebab bencana Deepwater Horizon awal tahun ini pada hari Rabu, dan meletakkan hampir semua kesalahan untuk letupan dan kemudiannya tumpahan minyak di dalam Teluk Mexico, tumpahan minyak di marin secara tidak sengaja terbesar di dalam sejarah industri petroleum, kepada, syarikat yang menguruskan rig.
foreignization:
Here's a translation of the text into Malay:

**BP mengeluarkan laporan mereka mengenai punca bencana Deepwater Horizon pada hari Rabu awal tahun ini, dan memindahkan sebahagian besar tanggungjawab bagi letupan dan tumpahan minyak berikutnya di Teluk Mexico, tumpahan minyak laut secara tidak sengaja terbesar dalam sejarah industri petroleum, kepada [nama syarikat pengurusan rig].**

**Explanation of choices:**

* **mengeluarkan laporan:** released their report
* **mengenai punca bencana:** regarding the causes of the disaster
* **pada hari Rabu awal tahun ini:** earlier this year on Wednesday
* **memindahkan sebahagian besar tanggungjawab:** shifted much of the blame
* **bagi letupan dan tumpahan minyak berikutnya:** for the explosion and subsequent oil spill
* **di Teluk Mexico:** in the Gulf of Mexico
* **tumpahan minyak laut secara tidak sengaja terbesar:** the largest accidental marine oil spill
* **dalam sejarah industri petroleum:** in the history of the petroleum industry
* **kepada [nama syarikat pengurusan rig]:** onto [the company managing the rig].  (I've left a space for the company name as it wasn't provided in the original text.)



**Alternative phrasing (slightly more formal):**

**BP telah menerbitkan laporan mengenai punca nahas Deepwater Horizon pada hari Rabu tahun ini, dan sebahagian besar menyalahkan [nama syarikat pengurusan rig] atas letupan dan tumpahan minyak yang menyusul di Teluk Mexico, yang merupakan tumpahan minyak laut terbesar secara tidak sengaja dalam sejarah industri petroleum.**

(This uses "menerbitkan" for "published" which is more formal than "mengeluarkan" and "menyalahkan" for "to blame".)

source: Earlier Itar-Tass also reported that according to an unnamed foreign diplomat in Pyongyang, Kim Jong Il's portraits have recently been taken down from public display secretly and without explanation.
domestication: Sebelum ini Itar-Tass turut melaporkan yang menurut seorang diplomat asing tidak dinamakan di Pyongyang, potret Kim Jong Il baru-baru ini telah diturunkan dari pameran awan secara rahsia dan tanpa sebarang penjelasan.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Sebelum ini, Itar-Tass juga melaporkan bahawa menurut seorang diplomat asing yang tidak dikenali di Pyongyang, potret Kim Jong Il telah diturunkan dari paparan awam secara senyap dan tanpa penjelasan."

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Terdahulu, Itar-Tass melaporkan bahawa seorang diplomat asing yang tidak dikenali di Pyongyang mendakwa potret Kim Jong Il baru-baru ini diturunkan dari tempat awam secara rahsia tanpa sebarang penjelasan."

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise and common phrasing):**

"Itar-Tass sebelum ini melaporkan, berdasarkan sumber diplomat asing di Pyongyang yang tidak dinamakan, potret Kim Jong Il telah diturunkan dari khalayak ramai secara senyap tanpa sebab."

**Explanation of choices:**

* **"Sebelum ini" / "Terdahulu"** - Both mean "Earlier"
* **"melaporkan"** - "reported"
* **"menurut" / "mendakwa" / "berdasarkan"** - All can mean "according to" or "claims".  "Mendakwa" implies a claim that might need verification. "Berdasarkan" is more neutral.
* **"diplomat asing yang tidak dikenali/dinamakan"** - "unnamed foreign diplomat" - "tidak dikenali" is more literal, "tidak dinamakan" is more common.
* **"potret"** - "portraits"
* **"paparan awam" / "tempat awam" / "khalayak ramai"** - "public display" - All are acceptable, "khalayak ramai" is slightly broader (public in general).
* **"secara senyap" / "secara rahsia"** - "secretly" - Both work.
* **"tanpa penjelasan" / "tanpa sebarang penjelasan" / "tanpa sebab"** - "without explanation" - All are correct.



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2 or 3** as they sound more natural in Malay.  **Option 3** is the most concise and commonly used phrasing.

source: The Icelandic internet bank, Icesave, has suspended all 300,000 of its UK customer accounts, leaving account holders in the United Kingdom unable to withdraw or deposit any funds.
domestication: Bank internet Iceland, Icesave, telah menggantung kesemua 300,000 akaun pelanggan UK, menyebabkan pemegang akaun di United Kingdom tidak dapat mengeluarkan atau mendepositkan apa-apa dana.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Bank internet Iceland, Icesave, telah menggantung semua 300,000 akaun pelanggannya di UK, menyebabkan pemegang akaun di United Kingdom tidak dapat mengeluarkan atau mendeposit sebarang wang."

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Icesave, sebuah bank internet dari Iceland, telah menangguhkan semua 300,000 akaun pelanggannya di UK.  Akibatnya, pelanggan di United Kingdom tidak boleh mengeluarkan atau mendeposit dana mereka."

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise and common phrasing):**

"Icesave, bank internet Iceland, telah menggantung operasi 300,000 akaun pelanggannya di UK, menyukarkan pelanggan di United Kingdom untuk mengeluarkan atau mendeposit wang."

**Explanation of Choices:**

* **"menggantung" vs. "menangguhkan":** Both mean "suspended," but "menangguhkan" is slightly more common in financial contexts.
* **"pelanggan" vs. "pemegang akaun":** Both mean "account holders," but "pelanggan" (customer) is more natural.
* **"dana mereka" vs. "sebarang wang":** Both mean "any funds," but "dana mereka" (their funds) sounds more natural in this context.
* **"menyukarkan" (Option 3):**  This translates to "making it difficult" and is a common way to express the consequence of a suspension.



**I recommend Option 2 or 3 as the most natural and clear translations.**  The best choice depends slightly on the specific context where this translation will be used.

source: "At the leadership of comrade Kim Jong-un, we have to change sadness to strength and courage and overcome today's difficulties," according to KCNA.

source: A 54-year-old German farmer who lost both arms in a farming accident six years ago has become the first patient to receive a complete double arm transplant.
domestication: Seorang petani Jerman berusia 54 tahun yang kehilangan kedua-dua tangannya dalam kemalangan semasa bertani enam tahun lalu telah menjadi pesakit pertama yang menerima pemindahan kedua-dua tangan yang lengkap.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for the Malay translation, ranging from more literal to more natural sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

Seorang petani Jerman berusia 54 tahun yang kehilangan kedua-dua belah tangan dalam kemalangan pertanian enam tahun lalu telah menjadi pesakit pertama menerima pemindahan tangan penuh berganda.

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

Seorang petani warga Jerman berusia 54 tahun yang kehilangan kedua-dua tangan akibat kemalangan di ladang enam tahun lalu, kini menjadi pesakit pertama yang menerima pembedahan transplantasi tangan penuh.

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise and common phrasing):**

Seorang petani Jerman berusia 54 tahun yang kehilangan kedua-dua tangannya dalam kemalangan pertanian enam tahun lalu, telah menerima pemindahan tangan penuh berganda buat pertama kali.

**Explanation of choices:**

* **"Seorang"** means "a" or "one".
* **"Petani"** means "farmer".
* **"Jerman"** means "German".
* **"berusia 54 tahun"** means "54 years old".
* **"kehilangan kedua-dua belah tangan"** or **"kehilangan kedua-dua tangan"** both mean "lost both arms".  "Belah" adds a little emphasis on 'both sides' but isn't always necessary.
* **"dalam kemalangan pertanian"** or **"akibat kemalangan di ladang"** means "in a farming accident" or "due to an accident on the farm".
* **"enam tahun lalu"** means "six years ago".
* **"pesakit pertama"** means "first patient".
* **"menerima"** means "to receive".
* **"pemindahan tangan penuh berganda"** or **"pembedahan transplantasi tangan penuh"** means "complete double arm transplant". "Pembedahan transplantasi" (transplant surgery) is a more common medical term.
* **"buat pertama kali"** means "for the first time".

**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2** as the most natural and commonly used way to express this idea in Malay.  It's clear, accurate, and sounds less like a direct translation.

source: Professor Andrew Collier Cameron of St. Andrew's University commented on the inherent problems with the planet: "The problem with this planet is that it's very massive and very close to its star."
domestication: Profeser Andrew Collier Cameron dari Universiti St. Andrew memberi komen bahawa masalah dengan kewujudan planet itu: "Masalah dengan planet itu ialah ianya sangat tumpat dan sangat dekat dengan bintang nya".
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Profesor Andrew Collier Cameron dari Universiti St. Andrew mengulas tentang masalah asas planet ini: 'Masalah dengan planet ini ialah ia sangat besar dan sangat dekat dengan bintangnya.'"

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Profesor Andrew Collier Cameron daripada Universiti St. Andrew memberikan komen mengenai masalah yang wujud pada planet ini: 'Masalah dengan planet ini adalah kerana ia terlalu besar dan terlalu dekat dengan bintangnya.'"

**Option 3 (Emphasis on the nature of the problem):**

"Profesor Andrew Collier Cameron dari Universiti St. Andrew menyatakan bahawa planet ini mempunyai masalah yang terangkum dalam saiz dan jaraknya: 'Masalah dengan planet ini ialah ia sangat besar dan sangat dekat dengan bintangnya.'"

**Explanation of choices:**

* **"dari" vs "daripada":** Both mean "from", but "dari" is generally more common and less formal.
* **"mengulas" vs "memberikan komen" vs "menyatakan":** These all translate to "commented," "gave a comment," or "stated" respectively.  "Mengulas" is the most direct translation.
* **"ialah" vs "adalah" vs "kerana":**  These all mean "is" or "because."  "Ialah" is very common, "adalah" is more formal, and "kerana" emphasizes the *reason* for the problem.
* **"terlalu"**: Added in Option 2 to emphasize "too" big and "too" close.
* **"terangkum dalam saiz dan jaraknya"**: In Option 3, this means "encapsulated in its size and distance," adding a bit more descriptive detail.



**I recommend Option 1 as the most natural and accurate translation for general use.**

source: Authorities evacuated an area that was one-half of a mile (approximately 800 m) wide.

**Option 2 (More Formal/Precise):**

   * This emphasizes the area being evacuated rather than the people.  *Dipindahkan* means "was evacuated" (passive voice).

**Option 3 (Slightly simpler):**

* **Pihak berkuasa telah mengosongkan kawasan seluas setengah batu (kira-kira 800 meter).**

   * *Mengosongkan* - to clear/evacuate.  This is a good alternative to *memindahkan* if you want a slightly less formal tone.

**Which one to use?**

* **Option 3** is a good alternative if you prefer a slightly simpler word for "evacuate."

source: HACAN describe themselves on their website as an "independent lobbying group which campaigns for quieter, cleaner and safer conditions for residents living under Heathrow's flight paths".
domestication: HACAN menggambarkan diri mereka di laman web mereka sebagai "kumpulan melobi bebas yang berkempen untuk keadaan lebih tenang, bersih dan selamat bagi penduduk di bawah laluan penerbangan Heathrow".
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"HACAN menerangkan diri mereka di laman web mereka sebagai sebuah "kumpulan lobi bebas yang berkempen untuk keadaan yang lebih senyap, bersih dan selamat bagi penduduk yang tinggal di laluan penerbangan Lapangan Terbang Heathrow"."

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"HACAN menggambarkan diri mereka dalam laman web mereka sebagai "kumpulan lobi merdeka yang memperjuangkan keadaan yang lebih tenang, bersih dan selamat untuk penduduk yang tinggal di bawah laluan penerbangan Lapangan Terbang Heathrow"."

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise and common phrasing):**

"Menurut laman web mereka, HACAN ialah "kumpulan lobi bebas yang berkempen untuk meningkatkan keadaan hidup yang lebih senyap, bersih dan selamat bagi penduduk di bawah laluan penerbangan Lapangan Terbang Heathrow"."

**Explanation of Choices:**

* **"menerangkan diri mereka" / "menggambarkan diri mereka" / "ialah"**:  These all translate "describe themselves".  "ialah" (is) is often used for a more concise and natural flow.
* **"kumpulan lobi bebas" / "kumpulan lobi merdeka"**: Both translate "independent lobbying group". "merdeka" is slightly more common for "independent" in this context.
* **"berkempen untuk" / "memperjuangkan"**: Both translate "campaigns for".  "memperjuangkan" can also imply advocating *for* something.
* **"keadaan yang lebih senyap, bersih dan selamat" / "keadaan yang lebih tenang, bersih dan selamat" / "keadaan hidup yang lebih senyap, bersih dan selamat"**:  These all translate "quieter, cleaner and safer conditions".  "tenang" is often used for "quiet" in the sense of peacefulness, while "senyap" is more about lack of noise.  Adding "hidup" (living) clarifies the conditions are related to quality of life.
* **"laluan penerbangan Lapangan Terbang Heathrow"**: This translates "Heathrow's flight paths".



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2 or 3** as they sound the most natural in Malay.  Option 3 is slightly more concise.

source: "All that metal right there, that's where they all were, there were 33 of them," said Elijah Kaneshiro, the owner's son.
domestication: "Semua logam yang di sana, itulah tempat mereka pernah berada, terdapat 33 kesemuanya," kata Elia Kaneshiro, anak pemilik.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, with slightly different nuances. The best choice depends on the desired level of formality and natural flow:

**Option 1 (Most Common & Natural):**

"Semua logam itu di sana, itulah tempat mereka semua berada. Ada 33 orang di sana," kata Elijah Kaneshiro, anak lelaki pemiliknya.

* **Explanation:** This is the most straightforward and commonly used translation.  "Semua logam itu di sana" translates "All that metal right there". "Itulah tempat mereka semua berada" translates "that's where they all were". "Ada 33 orang di sana" means "There were 33 people there."

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Semua logam di situ, itulah tempat mereka semua berada. Terdapat 33 orang di situ," kata Elijah Kaneshiro, anak lelaki pemiliknya.

* **Explanation:**  This is very similar to Option 1, but uses "terdapat" (there exist) which is a bit more formal than "ada" (there is/are).  "Di situ" is also slightly more formal than "di sana" for "there".

**Option 3 (Focusing on the location as the key point):**

"Di situlah semua logam itu berada, tempat 33 orang itu," kata Elijah Kaneshiro, anak lelaki pemiliknya.

* **Explanation:** This emphasizes the *location* as the key piece of information.  It's slightly less common in everyday speech but perfectly understandable.



**Key vocabulary used:**

* **Semua:** All
* **Logam:** Metal
* **Di sana / Di situ:** There (di sana is more common in casual speech, di situ is more formal)
* **Itulah:** That is
* **Tempat:** Place
* **Berada:** To be located/to be
* **Ada / Terdapat:** There is/are (terdapat is more formal)
* **Orang:** People/person
* **Kata:** Said
* **Anak lelaki:** Son
* **Pemiliknya:** The owner's



**Therefore, I recommend Option 1 as the most natural and commonly used translation:**

**"Semua logam itu di sana, itulah tempat mereka semua berada. Ada 33 orang di sana," kata Elijah Kaneshiro, anak lelaki pemiliknya.**

source: Coel Hellier of Keele University led a group of researchers that discovered the planet.
domestication: Coel Hellier dari Universiti Keele mengetuai kumpulan penyelidik yang menemui planet itu.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Coel Hellier of Keele University led a group of researchers that discovered the planet," depending on the desired nuance:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

* **Coel Hellier dari Universiti Keele mengetuai sebuah kumpulan penyelidik yang menemui planet tersebut.**

   * **Coel Hellier:** (Name remains the same)
   * **dari:** from
   * **Universiti Keele:** Keele University
   * **mengetuai:** led
   * **sebuah kumpulan:** a group of
   * **penyelidik:** researchers
   * **yang:** that/who
   * **menemui:** discovered
   * **planet tersebut:** the planet

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

* **Coel Hellier daripada Universiti Keele mengetuai satu pasukan penyelidik yang telah menjumpai planet itu.**

   * **daripada:** from (more formal than "dari")
   * **pasukan:** team (synonym for "kumpulan")
   * **telah menjumpai:** has discovered (slightly more emphasis on the completion of the discovery)
   * **planet itu:** that planet (slightly more definite than "planet tersebut")

**Option 3 (More concise):**

* **Coel Hellier, dari Universiti Keele, mengetuai penyelidik yang menemukan planet itu.**

   *  This uses a comma to separate the appositive phrase ("dari Universiti Keele").
   * **menemukan:** discovered (shorter and commonly used)

**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 is generally the best choice** for most contexts. It's clear, concise, and uses common Malay vocabulary.  

Therefore, I recommend:

**Coel Hellier dari Universiti Keele mengetuai sebuah kumpulan penyelidik yang menemui planet tersebut.**

source: On January 25, 2005, Don Brash, the leader of the New Zealand National Party made a speech to the Orewa Rotary Club where he focussed upon welfare dependency.
domestication: Pada 25 Januari, 2005, Don Brash, ketua kepada Party Kebangsaan New Zealand membuat ucapan kepada Kelab Rotari Orewa dimana dia memberi fokus kepada pergantungan kebajikan.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Pada 25 Januari 2005, Don Brash, pemimpin Parti Nasional New Zealand, telah menyampaikan ucapan kepada Kelab Rotary Orewa di mana beliau memberi tumpuan kepada pergantungan pada bantuan sosial."

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Pada 25 Januari 2005, Don Brash, ketua Parti Nasional New Zealand, berucap di hadapan Kelab Rotary Orewa dengan menekankan isu pergantungan terhadap bantuan sosial."

**Option 3 (Slightly more concise and common phrasing):**

"Don Brash, pemimpin Parti Nasional New Zealand, menyampaikan ucapan di Kelab Rotary Orewa pada 25 Januari 2005, yang menekankan isu pergantungan pada bantuan sosial."

**Explanation of choices:**

* **Pada:**  "On" (date)
* **Januari 25, 2005:** Remains the same (dates are usually not translated)
* **Don Brash:** Remains the same (proper name)
* **pemimpin/ketua:** Both mean "leader" - *pemimpin* is more general, *ketua* implies a more formal leadership role.
* **Parti Nasional New Zealand:** "New Zealand National Party" - Remains the same.
* **menyampaikan ucapan/berucap:** Both mean "made a speech" - *menyampaikan ucapan* is more formal, *berucap* is more common in everyday speech.
* **Kelab Rotary Orewa:** "Orewa Rotary Club" - Remains the same.
* **memberi tumpuan kepada / menekankan:** Both mean "focussed upon/emphasized"
* **pergantungan pada bantuan sosial / pergantungan terhadap bantuan sosial:** Both mean "welfare dependency" - *pada* and *terhadap* are both prepositions meaning "on/towards" and are interchangeable in this context.



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2** as it sounds the most natural and flows well in Malay.  However, all three options are grammatically correct and understandable.

source: The other ticket was sold at what is called the Favorite Market, located in Dalton, Georgia.
domestication: Tiket yang lain telah dijual di apa yang dikenali sebagai Pasaran kegemaran, bertempat di Dalton, Georgia.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "The other ticket was sold at what is called the Favorite Market, located in Dalton, Georgia." into Malay, with slight variations in nuance:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward):**

*   **Tiket yang satu lagi telah dijual di tempat yang dikenali sebagai Favorite Market, yang terletak di Dalton, Georgia.**

    *   *Tiket yang satu lagi* = The other ticket
    *   *telah dijual* = was sold
    *   *di tempat yang dikenali sebagai* = at a place known as / what is called
    *   *Favorite Market* = Favorite Market (name remains the same)
    *   *yang terletak di* = located in
    *   *Dalton, Georgia* = Dalton, Georgia

**Option 2 (Slightly more natural flow):**

*   **Tiket yang satu lagi dijual di Favorite Market, sebuah tempat yang terletak di Dalton, Georgia.**

    *   *sebuah tempat* = a place

**Option 3 (Focusing on the name):**

*   **Tiket yang satu lagi dijual di Favorite Market, yang berada di Dalton, Georgia.**

    *   *yang berada di* = which is in/located in

**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 1: Tiket yang satu lagi telah dijual di tempat yang dikenali sebagai Favorite Market, yang terletak di Dalton, Georgia.**  It is the most literal and clear translation, and accurately conveys the original meaning.

source: Last night, the California Aqueduct acted as a barrier to the fire.
domestication: Malam semalam, Akueduk California bertindak sebagai penyekat kebakaran tersebut.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Last night, the California Aqueduct acted as a barrier to the fire" into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

* **Malam semalam, Terusan California berfungsi sebagai penghalang kepada kebakaran itu.**

   * *Malam semalam* = Last night
   * *Terusan California* = California Aqueduct
   * *berfungsi sebagai* = acted as / functioned as
   * *penghalang* = barrier
   * *kepada* = to
   * *kebakaran itu* = the fire

**Option 2 (Slightly more descriptive, emphasizing the role):**

* **Terusan California bertindak sebagai penghalang kebakaran malam semalam.**

   * *Terusan California* = California Aqueduct
   * *bertindak sebagai* = acted as
   * *penghalang kebakaran* = fire barrier (combines "barrier" and "fire" into one phrase)
   * *malam semalam* = last night

**Option 3 (More formal):**

* **Terusan California telah bertindak sebagai suatu penghalang kepada kebakaran pada malam semalam.**

   * *Terusan California* = California Aqueduct
   * *telah bertindak sebagai* = had acted as (more formal)
   * *suatu* = a (adds a bit of emphasis)
   * *penghalang* = barrier
   * *kepada* = to
   * *kebakaran* = fire
   * *pada malam semalam* = on last night

**Which option is best?**

Option 1 is generally the most natural and commonly used.  Option 2 is also good and slightly more concise.  Option 3 is a bit more formal and might be suitable for a news report or official document.

Therefore, I recommend:

**Malam semalam, Terusan California berfungsi sebagai penghalang kepada kebakaran itu.**

source: Their study of WASP-18b was published in the August 27 issue of the journal Nature.
domestication: Penyelidikan mereka tentang WASP-18b telah disiarkan dalam jurnal Semula Jadi keluaran 27 Ogos.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Their study of WASP-18b was published in the August 27 issue of the journal Nature," with slight variations in formality and emphasis:

**Option 1 (Most common/natural):**

* **Kajian mereka mengenai WASP-18b diterbitkan dalam isu 27 Ogos jurnal Nature.**

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

* **Kajian tentang WASP-18b oleh mereka diterbitkan dalam keluaran 27 Ogos jurnal Nature.**

**Option 3 (Emphasizing the publication):**

* **Hasil kajian mereka mengenai WASP-18b telah diterbitkan dalam isu 27 Ogos jurnal Nature.** (Adding "hasil" - meaning "results" - emphasizes the findings were published)

**Breakdown of the key words:**

* **Their:**  *mereka* (most common), *oleh mereka* (by them - slightly more formal)
* **study:** *kajian*
* **of:** *mengenai* (about), *tentang* (about)
* **WASP-18b:** (remains the same - proper noun)
* **was published:** *diterbitkan* (was published), *telah diterbitkan* (has been published - slightly stronger emphasis)
* **in:** *dalam*
* **August 27 issue:** *isu 27 Ogos* (issue 27 August), *keluaran 27 Ogos* (edition 27 August)
* **journal:** *jurnal*
* **Nature:** (remains the same - proper noun)



**I recommend Option 1:  Kajian mereka mengenai WASP-18b diterbitkan dalam isu 27 Ogos jurnal Nature.**  It's the most natural and commonly used way to express this in Malay.

source: "Wikipedia is not a haven for dissidents but an encyclopedia."
domestication: "Wikipedia bukanlah syurga bagi penentang-penentang tetapi ensiklopedia".
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Wikipedia is not a haven for dissidents but an encyclopedia" into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most common/direct):**

**"Wikipedia bukanlah tempat perlindungan bagi pembangkang, tetapi sebuah ensiklopedia."**

*   **bukanlah:** is not
*   **tempat perlindungan:** haven/safe place/refuge
*   **bagi:** for
*   **pembangkang:** dissidents/opposition (can also mean rebels)
*   **tetapi:** but
*   **sebuah:** a (used for counting)
*   **ensiklopedia:** encyclopedia

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

**"Wikipedia bukanlah wadah bagi para pembangkang, melainkan sebuah ensiklopedia."**

*   **wadah:** container/platform/venue - a bit more formal than "tempat perlindungan"
*   **para:** a prefix used for pluralizing people (e.g., "para pembangkang" = the dissidents)
*   **melainkan:** rather/but (more formal than "tetapi")

**Option 3 (Emphasizing the *purpose* of Wikipedia):**

**"Wikipedia adalah sebuah ensiklopedia, bukan tempat bagi para pembangkang untuk berlindung."**

*   **adalah:** is
*   **untuk berlindung:** to take refuge/to shelter

**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 ("Wikipedia bukanlah tempat perlindungan bagi pembangkang, tetapi sebuah ensiklopedia.")** is the most natural and commonly used translation for this sentence.  It's clear, concise, and accurately conveys the original meaning.

source: The full impact of the change will be felt in the middle of the century.
domestication: Kesan penuh preubahan akan dapat dirasakan di pertengahan abad.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "The full impact of the change will be felt in the middle of the century" into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

* **Keseluruhan kesan perubahan itu akan dirasai pada pertengahan abad ini.** (This is a very direct and common translation. "Keseluruhan kesan" = full impact, "dirasai" = will be felt, "pertengahan abad ini" = middle of this century.)

* **Impak penuh perubahan tersebut akan terasa di pertengahan abad ini.** (Similar to the first, but uses "terasa" which also means "felt" and "tersebut" which means "the aforementioned/that" - slightly more formal.)

* **Perubahan itu akan memberi kesan sepenuhnya pada pertengahan abad ini.** (This focuses on the change *giving* the impact.  "akan memberi kesan sepenuhnya" = will have a full impact.)

**Recommendation:**

I'd recommend **Keseluruhan kesan perubahan itu akan dirasai pada pertengahan abad ini.** as the most natural and widely understood translation.

source: His death was announced by the Polish Defence Ministry and Lt. Sebastian Kostecki, a spokesman for the Polish military.
domestication: Kematiannya telah diumumkan oleh Kementerian Pertahanan Poland dan Lt. Sebastian Kostecki, jurucakap tentera Poland.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Kematiannya diumumkan oleh Kementerian Pertahanan Poland dan Leftenan Sebastian Kostecki, seorang jurucakap tentera Poland."

* **Kematiannya:** His death
* **diumumkan:** was announced
* **oleh:** by
* **Kementerian Pertahanan Poland:** Polish Defence Ministry
* **dan:** and
* **Leftenan Sebastian Kostecki:** Lt. Sebastian Kostecki
* **seorang jurucakap tentera Poland:** a spokesman for the Polish military

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Khabar kewafatan beliau telah disahkan oleh Kementerian Pertahanan Poland dan Leftenan Sebastian Kostecki, jurucakap rasmi tentera Poland."

* **Khabar kewafatan beliau:** News of his death (more formal than "kematiannya")
* **telah disahkan:** has been confirmed (slightly stronger than "diumumkan")
* **jurucakap rasmi:** official spokesman

**Option 3 (Focusing on the source of the announcement):**

"Kementerian Pertahanan Poland dan Leftenan Sebastian Kostecki, jurucakap tentera Poland, mengumumkan kematian beliau."

* **mengumumkan:** announced (active voice, focusing on who announced it)



**Which option is best?**

Option 1 is generally the most suitable for most contexts.  Option 2 is good for more formal reporting. Option 3 is also acceptable but changes the sentence structure slightly.

Therefore, I recommend:

**"Kematiannya diumumkan oleh Kementerian Pertahanan Poland dan Leftenan Sebastian Kostecki, seorang jurucakap tentera Poland."**

source: Recently Canadian MP Belinda Stronach had an affair with Tie Domi, a former NHL player.
domestication: Baru-baru ini Ahli Parlimen Kanada Belinda Stronach menmpunyai hubungan dengan Tie Domi, bekas pemain NHL.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Recently Canadian MP Belinda Stronach had an affair with Tie Domi, a former NHL player," into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Baru-baru ini, Ahli Parlimen Kanada Belinda Stronach menjalin hubungan sulit dengan Tie Domi, bekas pemain NHL."

* **Baru-baru ini:** Recently
* **Ahli Parlimen Kanada:** Canadian MP (Member of Parliament)
* **menjalin hubungan sulit:** had an affair (literally "established a difficult relationship") - this is the most common and polite way to say "affair" in Malay.
* **dengan:** with
* **Tie Domi:** Tie Domi
* **bekas pemain NHL:** former NHL player

**Option 2 (Slightly more direct, but still relatively polite):**

"Belinda Stronach, seorang Ahli Parlimen Kanada, baru-baru ini didakwa menjalin hubungan dengan Tie Domi, bekas pemain NHL."

* **didakwa menjalin hubungan:** was alleged to have an affair (adding "alleged" - *didakwa* - softens the statement)
* **seorang:** a (emphasizes she is an MP)

**Option 3 (More informal and direct - use with caution, as it's less polite):**

"Belinda Stronach, MP Kanada, baru-baru ini berselingkuh dengan Tie Domi, bekas pemain NHL."

* **berselingkuh:** to cheat, to have an affair (more direct and potentially harsher than *menjalin hubungan sulit*)

**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 is the best choice for most situations.** It's clear, accurate, and uses the most common and polite phrasing for "affair."  Option 2 is good if you want to add a degree of caution or indicate the information is unconfirmed.  Avoid Option 3 unless the context specifically calls for a very direct and potentially judgmental tone.

source: David Miliband, his brother and a fellow UK politician, was "the person I told after my mother", Ed stated; he "is delighted for me and he will obviously be there".
domestication: David Miliband, abangnya dan juga sahabat ahli politik UK, ialah "orang yang saya berithau selepas ibu saya," Ed menyatakan; dia "gembira untuk saya dan sudah jelas dia akan ada untuk saya."
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding. The best option depends on the desired tone and context:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"David Miliband, abangnya dan seorang lagi ahli politik UK, adalah "orang yang saya beritahu selepas ibu saya", kata Ed; dia "gembira untuk saya dan dia sudah tentu akan ada"."

* **Explanation:** This is a very direct translation, keeping the structure close to the English.  It uses "sudah tentu" for "obviously".

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Ed menyatakan bahawa David Miliband, abangnya dan juga rakan ahli politik dari UK, adalah "orang pertama yang saya beritahu selepas ibu saya".  David sangat gembira untuk saya dan dia pasti akan menyokong saya."

* **Explanation:** This sounds more natural in Malay.  It uses "orang pertama" (first person) instead of a direct equivalent of "the person I told".  "Pasti akan menyokong saya" is a more common way to say "he will obviously be there" in terms of support.

**Option 3 (Most Natural/Conversational):**

"Ed berkata, David Miliband, abangnya yang juga ahli politik di UK, ialah orang yang dia beritahu selepas ibunya.  David sangat gembira dengan berita ini dan dia pasti akan ada bersama saya."

* **Explanation:** This is the most fluid and natural-sounding option. It uses "berita ini" (this news) to clarify what David is happy about. "Ada bersama saya" (be with me) is a common and natural way to express support.



**Key vocabulary used:**

* **David Miliband:** David Miliband (name remains the same)
* **his brother:** abangnya
* **a fellow UK politician:** seorang lagi ahli politik UK / rakan ahli politik dari UK / ahli politik di UK
* **the person I told after my mother:** orang yang saya beritahu selepas ibu saya / orang pertama yang saya beritahu selepas ibu saya
* **Ed stated:** Ed menyatakan / Ed berkata
* **he is delighted for me:** dia gembira untuk saya / David sangat gembira dengan berita ini
* **he will obviously be there:** dia sudah tentu akan ada / dia pasti akan menyokong saya / dia pasti akan ada bersama

source: He heads Impossible2Possible, a nonprofit organization which advocates planet protection.
domestication: Beliau mengetuai Impossible2Possible, sebuah pertubuhan amal yang menggalakkan perlindungan planet.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "He heads Impossible2Possible, a nonprofit organization which advocates planet protection" into Malay, with slight variations in formality and emphasis:

**Option 1 (Most common/natural):**

"Beliau mengetuai Impossible2Possible, sebuah organisasi bukan untung yang memperjuangkan perlindungan planet."

* **Beliau:** He (formal/respectful)
* **mengetuai:** heads/leads
* **Impossible2Possible:** (remains the same as it's a name)
* **sebuah organisasi bukan untung:** a nonprofit organization
* **yang memperjuangkan:** which advocates/champions
* **perlindungan planet:** planet protection

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Ia mengetuai Impossible2Possible, sebuah organisasi bukan untung yang menyokong usaha perlindungan planet."

* **Ia:** He (more neutral, can also mean "it")
* **menyokong usaha:** supports efforts (slightly different nuance than "advocates")

**Option 3 (More concise):**

"Beliau adalah ketua Impossible2Possible, sebuah organisasi bukan untung yang memperjuangkan pemeliharaan planet."

* **adalah ketua:** is the head/leader
* **pemeliharaan planet:** planet conservation (similar to protection)

**Which option is best?**

Option 1 is generally the best and most natural-sounding translation for most contexts.  The choice between "beliau" and "ia" depends on the level of formality desired.  "Memperjuangkan" is a strong word that effectively conveys "advocates."

source: A statement by the group read, "The Ulster Defense Association was formed to defend our communities."
domestication: Satu kenyataan oleh kumpulan tersebut berkata, "Kesatuan Pertahanan Ulster telah ditubuhkan untuk mempertahankan masyarakat kita."
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English sentence into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Satu kenyataan daripada kumpulan itu menyebut, "Persatuan Pertahanan Ulster telah ditubuhkan untuk mempertahankan komuniti kami.""

*   **Satu kenyataan:** A statement
*   **daripada kumpulan itu:** from the group
*   **menyebut:** stated/read (literally "mentioned")
*   **Persatuan Pertahanan Ulster:** Ulster Defense Association
*   **telah ditubuhkan:** was formed
*   **untuk mempertahankan:** to defend
*   **komuniti kami:** our communities

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Dalam satu kenyataan, kumpulan itu menyatakan, "Persatuan Pertahanan Ulster ditubuhkan bagi membela komuniti kami.""

*   **Dalam satu kenyataan:** In a statement
*   **kumpulan itu menyatakan:** the group stated
*   **bagi membela:** in order to defend (more formal than "untuk mempertahankan")

**Option 3 (Focus on purpose):**

"Kumpulan itu mengeluarkan kenyataan yang berbunyi, "Persatuan Pertahanan Ulster dibentuk untuk melindungi komuniti kami.""

*   **Kumpulan itu mengeluarkan kenyataan:** The group issued a statement
*   **yang berbunyi:** which reads/said
*   **dibentuk:** was formed (synonym of "ditubuhkan")
*   **untuk melindungi:** to protect (synonym of "mempertahankan" and "membela")



**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 ("Satu kenyataan daripada kumpulan itu menyebut...") is the most natural and widely understood translation.**  It's a good balance of accuracy and clarity.

source: This report has been confirmed by some expatriates living in the country.
domestication: Laporan ini telah disahkan oleh seorang pekerja asing yang tinggal didalam negara tersebut.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "This report has been confirmed by some expatriates living in the country" into Malay, with slight nuances:

**Option 1 (Most common & straightforward):**

* **Laporan ini telah disahkan oleh beberapa ekspatriat yang tinggal di negara ini.**

   * **Laporan ini:** This report
   * **telah disahkan:** has been confirmed (passive voice)
   * **oleh:** by
   * **beberapa:** some
   * **ekspatriat:** expatriates
   * **yang tinggal:** who live/living
   * **di negara ini:** in this country

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

* **Kebenaran laporan ini telah disahkan oleh beberapa warga asing yang menetap di negara ini.**

   * **Kebenaran laporan ini:** The truth/accuracy of this report
   * **telah disahkan:** has been confirmed
   * **oleh:** by
   * **beberapa:** some
   * **warga asing:** foreigners (another way to say expatriates, slightly more general)
   * **yang menetap:** who reside/are settled
   * **di negara ini:** in this country

**Option 3 (Focuses on verification):**

* **Beberapa ekspatriat yang tinggal di negara ini telah mengesahkan laporan ini.**

   * **Beberapa ekspatriat:** Some expatriates
   * **yang tinggal:** who live/living
   * **di negara ini:** in this country
   * **telah mengesahkan:** have verified/confirmed (active voice - they did the confirming)
   * **laporan ini:** this report

**Which one to use?**

* **Option 1 is the most generally applicable and natural-sounding.**
* **Option 2** is good if you want to emphasize the *truth* of the report.  "Warga asing" is a broader term than "ekspatriat."
* **Option 3** is good if you want to emphasize that the expatriates *actively* verified the report.



Therefore, I recommend:  **Laporan ini telah disahkan oleh beberapa ekspatriat yang tinggal di negara ini.**

source: Officials report that his body was taken overnight from the Deftera Cemetery in Nicosia.
domestication: Para pegawai melaporkan bahawa mayatnya diambil semalaman daripada Perkuburan Deftera di Nicosia.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating "Officials report that his body was taken overnight from the Deftera Cemetery in Nicosia," depending on the nuance you want to convey:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

* **Pegawai melaporkan bahawa mayatnya telah diambil dari Tanah Perkuburan Deftera di Nicosia sepanjang malam.**

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

* **Pihak berkuasa melaporkan mayat beliau telah dibawa dari Tanah Perkuburan Deftera di Nicosia sepanjang malam.**

**Option 3 (Emphasizing the removal/theft):**

* **Pegawai melaporkan mayatnya telah dicuri dari Tanah Perkuburan Deftera di Nicosia sepanjang malam.** (This uses "dicuri" which means "stolen" - useful if the implication is theft)

**Breakdown of the words:**

* **Pegawai / Pihak berkuasa:** Officials/Authorities
* **melaporkan:** report
* **bahawa:** that
* **mayatnya / mayat beliau:** his body ( *mayatnya* is more common, *beliau* is more formal/respectful)
* **telah diambil / telah dibawa / telah dicuri:** was taken / was removed / was stolen
* **dari:** from
* **Tanah Perkuburan Deftera:** Deftera Cemetery
* **di Nicosia:** in Nicosia
* **sepanjang malam:** overnight / throughout the night



**Recommendation:**  **Option 1 (Pegawai melaporkan bahawa mayatnya telah diambil dari Tanah Perkuburan Deftera di Nicosia sepanjang malam.)** is the most natural and generally applicable translation.  Choose Option 3 if the context clearly indicates the body was stolen.

source: The judge overseeing the bail proceedings, ruled that Parasiris would not pose an immediate threat to society, but did place strict conditions on his release.
domestication: Hakim mengawasi prosiding jaminan, memutuskan bahawa Parasiris tidak akan menimbulkan ancaman segera kepada masyarakat, tetapi hanya mengenakan syarat yang lebih ketat ke atas pembebasannya.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English sentence into Malay, with slightly different nuances. The best choice depends on the specific context and desired formality:

**Option 1 (Most common/natural):**

"Hakim yang mengendalikan prosiding jaminan itu memutuskan bahawa Parasiris tidak akan menimbulkan ancaman segera kepada masyarakat, tetapi tetap mengenakan syarat-syarat ketat ke atas pembebasannya."

* **Hakim:** Judge

source: While American Association of Retired Persons (AARP) Policy Director John Rother concedes that many of the arguments of opponents are valid, price indexing is inevitable and must be viewed in the context of President Bush’s total reform package.

source: The Jordanian newspaper Al-Dustur reported that some Middle Eastern TV stations were broadcasting images of Korans shredded by US forces in one Iraqi mosque.

foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"Kertas akhbar Jordan, Al-Dustur, melaporkan bahawa beberapa stesen televisyen di Timur Tengah menyiarkan imej-imej al-Quran yang dikoyak-koyak oleh tentera AS di sebuah masjid Iraq."

**Option 3 (Emphasis on the act being disrespectful):**

"Kertas akhbar Jordan, Al-Dustur, melaporkan bahawa beberapa stesen televisyen di Timur Tengah menyiarkan imej al-Quran yang dituduh dikoyak-koyak oleh tentera AS di sebuah masjid Iraq." (Adding "dituduh" - accused - subtly implies the report is potentially contentious)

**Breakdown of the translation:**

* **reported:** melaporkan (reported)
* **that:** bahawa (that)
* **some Middle Eastern TV stations:** beberapa stesen televisyen di Timur Tengah
* **were broadcasting:** menyiarkan (broadcasting)
* **images of Korans:** imej-imej al-Quran / gambar-gambar al-Quran (images of the Quran)
* **shredded:** dikoyak-koyak (torn to pieces) / dicincang (chopped up - can also imply shredding)
* **by US forces:** oleh tentera AS / oleh tentera Amerika Syarikat
* **in one Iraqi mosque:** di sebuah masjid Iraq / di sebuah masjid di Iraq



**Which option to choose?**

* **Option 1** is the most natural and generally the best choice.
* **Option 2** is slightly more formal and uses "gambar-gambar" for images, which is also perfectly acceptable.
* **Option 3** is useful if you want to subtly indicate that the report itself might be disputed.

source: Following confirmation by Thailand's caretaker cabinet, a proposal to create a 77th province to honour the king's 80th birthday, will be the subject of a feasibility study.
domestication: Berikutan pengesahan oleh kabinet sementara Thailand, satu cadangan untuk mewujudkan sebuah daerah ke-77 untuk memberi penghormatan ulang tahun ke-80 raja, akan menjadi subjek kajian kebolehlaksanaan.
foreignization:

"Cadangan mewujudkan wilayah ke-77 bagi memperingati ulang tahun ke-80 Raja akan dikaji kelayakannya, susulan pengesahan kabinet sementara Thailand."



**Explanation of choices:**

* **"Susulan" / "Selepas"**: Both mean "following" or "after". "Selepas" is slightly more common in everyday speech.
* **"kabinet sementara" / "kabinet penjaga"**: Both translate to "caretaker cabinet." "Penjaga" is often used in this context.
* **"mewujudkan"**: Means "to create" or "to establish".
* **"wilayah ke-77"**:  "77th province" - this is straightforward.
* **"bagi memperingati" / "sempena memperingati"**: Both mean "to honour" or "in celebration of". "Sempena" is a bit more formal and often used for celebrations.
* **"ulang tahun ke-80 hari lahir Raja" / "hari lahir ke-80 Raja"**: Both mean "the king's 80th birthday". The latter is more concise.
* **"kajian kemungkinan" / "dikaji dari segi kelayakannya" / "dikaji kelayakannya"**: All mean "feasibility study" or "will be studied for its feasibility".  The last option is the most concise.



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2:**  **"Selepas disahkan oleh kabinet penjaga Thailand, cadangan mewujudkan wilayah ke-77 sempena memperingati hari lahir ke-80 Raja akan dikaji dari segi kelayakannya."**  It sounds the most natural and is commonly used

**Option 3 (Focusing on the result):**

"Chris Latham kemudiannya menjaringkan cubaan kedua dalam perlawanan, menjadikan ia cubaan keempat Australia, yang membantu mereka mengukuhkan kedudukan dan memperoleh mata bonus."

**Breakdown of key words:**

*   **scored:** menjaringkan (to score)
*   **try:** cubaan (in rugby/sports)
*   **restore:** mengembalikan (to restore/regain)
*   **hold of the game:** penguasaan dalam permainan / keunggulan dalam permainan (control of the game/advantage in the game)
*   **secure:** memastikan / meraih / memperoleh (to secure/to obtain)
*   **bonus point:** mata bonus (bonus point)



**Which option is best?**

Option 2 ("Namun, Chris Latham menambah cubaan kedua untuk dirinya dan cubaan keempat untuk Australia, sekali gus mengembalikan keunggulan mereka dalam permainan dan meraih mata bonus.") is likely the most natural-sounding and commonly used in sports reporting in Malay.  It flows well and accurately conveys the meaning.

foreignization:
Here are a few options for the Malay translation, depending on the nuance you want to convey. I've included explanations to help you choose:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

**Di daerah Muang, Nakhon Sri Thammarat, banyak jalan telah ditutup dan beg pasir sedang digunakan untuk membantu perniagaan yang terjejas.**

* **Di daerah Muang:** In Muang District
* **Nakhon Sri Thammarat:**  (remains the same - proper noun)
* **banyak jalan telah ditutup:** many roads have been closed
* **dan beg pasir sedang digunakan:** and sandbags are being deployed/used
* **untuk membantu perniagaan yang terjejas:** to help affected businesses.  ("terjejas" means affected/impacted)

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal, emphasizes the action of deploying):**

**Di daerah Muang, Nakhon Sri Thammarat, banyak jalan telah ditutup dan beg pasir sedang ditempatkan untuk membantu perniagaan yang terjejas.**

* **ditempatkan** means "placed" or "positioned" - slightly more specific than "digunakan" (used).

**Option 3 (More natural sounding, slightly less literal):**

**Pelbagai jalan di daerah Muang, Nakhon Sri Thammarat telah ditutup, dan beg pasir disediakan untuk membantu peniaga yang terjejas.**

* **Pelbagai jalan:** Various roads (sounds more natural than "banyak jalan" in some contexts)
* **peniaga:**  traders/business owners (more specific than "perniagaan" - businesses - but often used interchangeably in this context)
* **disediakan:** provided/made available. This implies the sandbags are ready for use.



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 1:  Di daerah Muang, Nakhon Sri Thammarat, banyak jalan telah ditutup dan beg pasir sedang digunakan untuk membantu perniagaan yang terjejas.**  It's clear, concise, and commonly used phrasing.

source: The HRCP also said that in almost all cases of violence against journalists in the last few years, those responsible have not been identified or punished.
domestication: HRCP juga berkata bahawa dalam hampir semua kes-kes keganasan terhadap wartawan dalam beberapa tahun yang lalu, mereka yang bertanggungjawab tidak dikenal pasti atau dihukum.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, with slightly different nuances:

**Option 1 (Most straightforward and common):**

"HRCP juga menyatakan bahawa dalam hampir semua kes keganasan terhadap wartawan dalam beberapa tahun kebelakangan ini, mereka yang bertanggungjawab tidak dikenali atau dihukum."

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"HRCP turut memaklumkan bahawa dalam kebanyakkan kes keganasan terhadap wartawan sejak beberapa tahun lalu, pihak yang bertanggungjawab tidak dapat dikenal pasti atau diberi hukuman."

**Option 3 (Focusing on lack of accountability):**

"HRCP juga berkata bahawa dalam hampir semua kes keganasan terhadap wartawan dalam beberapa tahun kebelakangan ini, tiada siapa yang bertanggungjawab dikenali atau dihukum."

**Breakdown of the key phrases:**

*   **HRCP:** HRCP (no change, it's an acronym)
*   **also said:** juga menyatakan / turut memaklumkan / juga berkata
*   **in almost all cases:** dalam hampir semua kes / dalam kebanyakkan kes
*   **violence against journalists:** keganasan terhadap wartawan
*   **in the last few years:** dalam beberapa tahun kebelakangan ini / sejak beberapa tahun lalu
*   **those responsible:** mereka yang bertanggungjawab / pihak yang bertanggungjawab / tiada siapa yang bertanggungjawab
*   **have not been identified:** tidak dikenali / tidak dapat dikenal pasti
*   **or punished:** atau dihukum / atau diberi hukuman



**Recommendation:**

**Option 1** is the most natural and commonly used way to express this in Malay. It's clear and concise.  However, the best option depends on the overall context of the document.

source: Major British charity Comic Relief has invested money in arms, alcohol and tobacco firms, according to a BBC Panorama investigation to be broadcast this evening.

**Option 3 (Most Natural - and likely how a news report would phrase it):**

"Comic Relief, sebuah pertubuhan amal utama di Britain, didapati melabur wang dalam syarikat senjata, alkohol dan tembakau, menurut siasatan oleh BBC Panorama yang akan disiarkan malam ini."

**Explanation of choices:**

* **Pertubuhan amal:** Charity/Organization
* **British utama:** Major British
* **melabur wang:** invested money
* **syarikat:** firms/companies
* **senjata:** arms/weapons
* **alkohol:** alcohol
* **tembakau:** tobacco
* **menurut:** according to
* **siasatan:** investigation
* **disiarkan:** to be broadcast
* **petang ini / malam ini:** this evening (both are acceptable, "malam ini" is slightly more common for evening broadcasts)
* **didapati:** found to be (used in Option 3 for a more natural flow)
* **terlibat dalam:** involved in (used in Option 2 for a slightly different phrasing)



**Recommendation:**  I recommend **Option 3** as it sounds the most natural and reads like a typical news headline or opening sentence in Malay.  It’s concise and clear.

source: As a result of consumer's concerns, footwear companies have been diverting financial and human resources towards developing and implementing better codes of conduct for suppliers.

    *  "sumber kewangan dan tenaga kerja" - financial and human resources
    *  "untuk membangun dan melaksanakan" - to develop and implement
    *  "kod amalan" - codes of conduct
    *  "yang lebih baik" - better
    *  "bagi pembekal mereka" - for their suppliers

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Berikutan kebimbangan yang ditimbulkan oleh para pengguna, firma-firma kasut telah menyalurkan sumber kewangan dan manusia untuk membangun dan melaksanakan kod kelakuan yang lebih baik untuk pembekal."

* **Explanation:**  Uses slightly more formal vocabulary.
    * "Berikutan" - Following/As a consequence of
    * "ditimbulkan oleh para pengguna" - raised by consumers (more formal than just "kebimbangan pengguna")
    * "firma-firma kasut" - footwear firms (more formal than "syarikat-syarikat kasut")
    * "menyalurkan" - channeling (a slightly more nuanced way to say "diverting")
    * "kod kelakuan" - code of conduct (synonymous with "kod amalan")

**Option 3 (Focus on addressing concerns):**

"Untuk menangani kebimbangan pengguna, syarikat-syarikat kasut telah mengarahkan sumber kewangan dan tenaga kerja ke arah membangun dan melaksanakan kod amalan yang lebih baik untuk pembekal mereka."

* **Explanation:**  Emphasizes the *purpose* of the diversion.
    * "Untuk menangani" - To address/To handle
    * "mengarahkan" - directing/steering



**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 is generally the best choice for most contexts

source: The conservative opposition Partido Popular has focused his campaign in the terrorism and separatism, especially that of the Basque.

foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text into Malay, with slightly different nuances:

"Parti Popular, pembangkang konservatif, telah menumpukan kempen mereka terhadap keganasan dan pemisahan, terutamanya yang melibatkan Basque."

*   **Parti Popular:** Partido Popular (name remains the same)
*   **pembangkang konservatif:** conservative opposition
*   **telah menumpukan kempen mereka:** has focused their campaign
*   **terhadap:** on/against

*   **terutamanya:** especially
*   **yang melibatkan Basque:** that of the Basque (literally "that involving Basque")

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Parti Popular, parti pembangkang yang konservatif, memberi tumpuan kepada kempen mereka mengenai keganasan dan gerakan pemisahan diri, khususnya yang berkaitan dengan Basque."

*   **parti pembangkang yang konservatif:**  a conservative opposition party (slightly more descriptive)
*   **memberi tumpuan kepada:** gives focus to
*   **mengenai:** regarding/on
*   **gerakan pemisahan diri:** separatist movement (a slightly more descriptive term for separatism)
*   **khususnya:** specifically
*   **yang berkaitan dengan Basque:** related to Basque

**Option 3 (More concise):**

"Parti Popular, pembangkang konservatif, menumpukan kempen pada isu keganasan dan pemisahan, terutama Basque."

*   **isu:** issue (can be used to make it more concise)
*   **terutama:** primarily/mainly (similar to "especially")



**Which one to use?**

*   **Option 1** is the most direct and likely the best choice for general use.
*   **Option 2** is suitable for more formal writing.
*   **Option 3** is good if you need to be brief.



Therefore, I recommend:  **"Parti Popular, pembangkang konservatif, telah menumpukan kempen mereka terhadap keganasan dan pemisahan, terutamanya yang melibatkan Basque."**

source: "Transocean's investigation is ongoing, and will be concluded when all of the evidence is in, including the critical information the company has requested of BP but has yet to receive."
domestication: "Penyiasatan Transocean sedang dilaksanakan, dan akan disimpulkan apabila semua bukti telah ada, termasuklah maklumat kritikal yang syarikat tersebut telah meminta daripada BP tetapi masih belum menerimanya."
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding. The best option depends on the specific context and desired tone:

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Penyelidikan Transocean masih berjalan, dan akan diselesaikan apabila semua bukti telah diterima, termasuk maklumat kritikal yang syarikat itu telah minta daripada BP tetapi belum diterimanya."

* **Pros:** Very accurate to the original wording.
* **Cons:** Can sound slightly formal and a bit wordy.

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Penyelidikan oleh Transocean masih dijalankan dan akan disempurnakan setelah semua bukti diperoleh, termasuk maklumat penting yang telah diminta daripada BP tetapi masih belum diterima."

* **Pros:** Flows more naturally in Malay.  Uses "dijalankan" (being conducted) and "disempurnakan" (completed) which are common phrases.
* **Cons:** Slightly less literal than Option 1.

**Option 3 (Concise and Common):**

"Transocean masih menyiasat perkara ini dan penyelidikan akan selesai sebaik sahaja semua bukti diterima, termasuk maklumat penting yang dipohon daripada BP tetapi masih belum diperoleh."

* **Pros:**  Concise and uses common Malay vocabulary ("menyiasat" - investigating, "dipohon" - requested, "diperoleh" - obtained).
* **Cons:**  Slightly less emphasis on the ongoing nature of the investigation.

**Breakdown of Key Words:**

* **Transocean:** Transocean (remains the same)
* **investigation:** penyelidikan (investigation) / menyiasat (to investigate)
* **is ongoing:** masih berjalan / masih dijalankan / masih menyiasat
* **will be concluded:** akan diselesaikan / akan disempurnakan / akan selesai
* **when:** apabila / sebaik sahaja
* **all of the evidence:** semua bukti
* **is in:** telah diterima / diperoleh
* **critical information:** maklumat kritikal / maklumat penting
* **the company has requested of BP:** syarikat itu telah minta daripada BP / telah diminta daripada BP / dipohon daripada BP
* **but has yet to receive:** tetapi belum diterimanya / tetapi masih belum diterima / tetapi masih belum diperoleh



**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 2** or **Option 3** as they sound the most natural and are commonly used in

source: It burned 500 acres south of the 14 Freeway, but the Los Angeles County Fire Department (LACFD) now has it contained, and firefighters are hopeful that it will burn itself out as it edges closer to the 160,577-acre burn area of last year's Station Fire.
domestication: Ia membakar 500 ekar selatan lebuh raya 14, tetapi Jabatan Bomba Daerah Los Angeles (LACFD) kini telah menghentikannya, dan ahli bomba berharap ia akan mati sendiri berikutan ia mendekati 160,577-ekar kawasan bakar Station Fire tahun lepas.
foreignization:
Here's a translation of the text into Malay:

**Ia membakar seluas 202 hektar (500 ekar) di selatan Lebuhraya 14, tetapi Jabatan Bomba Kaunti Los Angeles (LACFD) kini telah mengawalnya, dan bomba optimis bahawa ia akan padam sendiri ketika menghampiri kawasan kebakaran seluas 65,000 hektar (160,577 ekar) daripada kebakaran Stesen tahun lalu.**

Here's a breakdown of the translation choices:

* **Ia:** It
* **membakar:** burned/burning
* **seluas:** covering an area of/amounting to
* **202 hektar (500 ekar):**  I included both hectares (standard metric) and acres (as in the original) for clarity.  1 acre = approximately 0.405 hectares.
* **di selatan:** south of
* **Lebuhraya 14:** Freeway 14 (Lebuhraya is a common translation for "freeway" or "highway")
* **tetapi:** but
* **Jabatan Bomba Kaunti Los Angeles (LACFD):** Los Angeles County Fire Department (using the acronym is common)
* **kini telah mengawalnya:** now has it contained (mengawal = to control)
* **dan bomba optimis:** and firefighters are hopeful/optimistic (bomba = firefighters)
* **bahawa ia akan padam sendiri:** that it will burn itself out (padam = to extinguish/to go out)
* **ketika menghampiri:** as it edges closer to
* **kawasan kebakaran:** burn area/fire area
* **seluas 65,000 hektar (160,577 ekar):** Again, hectares and acres are given.
* **daripada kebakaran Stesen tahun lalu:** of last year's Station Fire (Kebakaran Stesen = Station Fire)

source: The decision was well received across Somalia, as hundreds of thousands of farmers heavily rely on animal exports to the oil-rich Middle East.

foreignization:

* **Keputusan itu:** The decision
* **disambut baik:** was well received
* **di seluruh Somalia:** across Somalia
* **kerana:** because / as
* **ratusan ribu:** hundreds of thousands
* **petani:** farmers
* **sangat bergantung pada:** heavily rely on
* **eksport haiwan:** animal exports
* **ke:** to
* **Timur Tengah:** Middle East
* **yang kaya dengan minyak:** oil-rich

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Keputusan tersebut telah diterima dengan baik di Somalia, memandangkan ratusan ribu petani amat bergantung kepada pengeluaran haiwan ke negara-negara Timur Tengah yang kaya dengan minyak."

* **Keputusan tersebut:** The decision (more formal than "itu")
* **telah diterima dengan baik:** has been well received (slightly more formal than "disambut baik")
* **memandangkan:** considering / given that

**Option 3 (More conversational):**

"Orang ramai di Somalia menyambut baik keputusan ini, sebab ramai sangat petani bergantung pada jualan haiwan ke negara-negara Timur Tengah yang banyak minyak."

* **Orang ramai:** People (more general than specifically farmers)

* **jualan haiwan:** sale of animals (more conversational than "eksport haiwan")
* **banyak minyak:** a lot of oil (more conversational than "kaya dengan minyak")



**Recommendation:**

**Option 1 is the best general translation.** It's clear, concise, and uses commonly understood Malay vocabulary.  The other options are good too, depending on the context and the desired level of formality.

source: Robert Mugabe, the Zimbabwean president who has recently been widely criticized by world leaders, has claimed the current results show that he will have a 'sweeping victory' in the unopposed presidential run-off elections.

    * `anak lelaki pasangan Texas` - the son of a Texas couple

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

* **Explanation:**  Uses `ditahan` (detained) which can sound slightly more formal than `ditangkap` (arrested).  `atas tuduhan menyamar` (on charges of impersonating) is also a bit more legalistic.

**Recommendation:**

I recommend **Option 1** as the most natural and commonly understood translation.  It's clear, concise, and accurately conveys the meaning of the original English sentence.

source: Wikipedia has been blocked twice before, the first from 2-21 June 2004, due to the 15th anniversary of the Tian'anmen Square Incident.
domestication: Wikipedia telah dihalang dua kali sebelum ini, pertamanya dari 2-21 Jun 2004, akibat ulang tahun ke-15 Kejadian Tian'anmen Square.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for the Malay translation, depending on the level of formality and nuance you want to convey:

**Option 1 (Most Common/Neutral):**

"Wikipedia telah disekat sebanyak dua kali sebelum ini, pertama dari 2-21 Jun 2004, disebabkan ulang tahun ke-15 insiden Dataran Tiananmen."

**Option 2 (Slightly more formal):**

"Wikipedia pernah disekat dua kali sebelum ini, iaitu dari 2 hingga 21 Jun 2004, bersempena dengan ulang tahun ke-15 peristiwa Dataran Tiananmen."

**Option 3 (Very literal, potentially less natural):**

"Wikipedia telah disekat dua kali dahulu, pertama dari 2-21 Jun 2004, kerana ulang tahun ke-15 kejadian Dataran Tiananmen."

**Explanation of choices:**

* **disekat:**  The most common word for "blocked" in this context.
* **sebanyak dua kali / dua kali:** Both mean "twice."  "Sebanyak" adds a bit of emphasis.
* **sebelum ini / dahulu:** Both mean "before." "Sebelum ini" is more common in modern Malay.
* **disebabkan / kerana / bersempena dengan:** All mean "due to" or "because of." "Bersempena dengan" is more formal and implies an event being *in connection with* something.
* **insiden / peristiwa / kejadian:** All mean "incident" or "event." "Insiden" is often used for specific, often negative, events.  "Peristiwa" is more general. "Kejadian" is the most literal translation of "incident" but can sound slightly less natural in this context.
* **Dataran Tiananmen:**  The standard Malay translation of "Tiananmen Square."



**I recommend Option 1: "Wikipedia telah disekat sebanyak dua kali sebelum ini, pertama dari 2-21 Jun 2004, disebabkan ulang tahun ke-15 insiden Dataran Tiananmen."**  It's the most natural and commonly used way to express this information in Malay.

source: But the right-hander wasn't needed two nights after giving up Albert Pujols' three-run homer with two outs in the ninth inning that gave the Cardinals a 5-4 victory and extended the NLCS.
domestication: Tetapi pemain yang menggunakan tangan kanan itu tidak diperlukan selepas dua malam selepas melepaskan peluang 'tiga-run homer dengan dua out Albert Pujols dalam inning kesembilan yang memberikan Kardinal kemenangan 5-4 dan memperluaskan NLCS.
foreignization:
Here are a few options for translating the English text to Malay, ranging from more literal to more natural-sounding. The best option depends on the context and target audience.

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Namun, pemain kanan itu tidak diperlukan dua malam selepas melepaskan homer tiga larian Albert Pujols dengan dua out pada inning kesembilan yang memberikan Cardinals kemenangan 5-4 dan melanjutkan siri NLCS."

* **Pros:** Very close to the original English structure.
* **Cons:** Might sound a little clunky to native Malay speakers.  Uses loanwords ("homer," "inning," "NLCS") which are common in sports reporting but could be less accessible to a general audience.

**Option 2 (More Natural):**

"Walau bagaimanapun, pemain kanan itu tidak dipanggil untuk bermain dua malam selepas dia membenarkan Albert Pujols melakukan pukulan tiga mata dengan dua out pada inning kesembilan, yang memberi kemenangan 5-4 kepada Cardinals dan memanjangkan siri NLCS."

* **Pros:** Flows more naturally in Malay.  Uses "membenarkan" (allowed) which is a good equivalent for "giving up" in this context.
* **Cons:** Still uses loanwords ("homer," "inning," "NLCS").

**Option 3 (Most Natural - and slightly longer, explaining terms if needed):**

"Walau bagaimanapun, pemain kanan itu tidak diperlukan untuk bermain dua malam selepas dia membenarkan Albert Pujols melakukan pukulan *home run* tiga mata dengan dua out pada *inning* kesembilan, yang memberi kemenangan 5-4 kepada Cardinals dan memanjangkan siri *National League Championship Series* (NLCS)."

* **Pros:** Most natural sounding.  Includes explanations of the loanwords in parentheses for clarity, which is helpful for a broader audience.
* **Cons:**  Longer than the other options.

**Key Vocabulary Notes:**

* **Right-hander:** *pemain kanan* (right-handed player)
* **Giving up:** *melepaskan* (to release, to allow - in this context) or *membenarkan* (to allow)
* **Three-run homer:** *homer tiga larian* or *pukulan tiga mata* (three-point hit/shot - a common way to describe a home run with runners on base)
* **Two outs:** *dua out*
